workEthic.title: How I work
workEthic.paragraph1: I'm a software engineer who takes both life and work seriously. I naturally build strong connections wherever I go — I enjoy getting close to the people I work with and working as a true team, built on trust and synergy. At the same time, I never lose sight of what matters most to me: respect, truth, and kindness — values that are non-negotiable in how I work and collaborate.
workEthic.paragraph2: I care deeply about what I build, and I naturally push myself to go the extra mile. Not because I have to, but because I believe things should be done well. Some might see it as intensity; my teammates usually experience it as raising the bar — delivering quality, moving things forward, and creating momentum within the team.
workEthic.paragraph3: Without really trying to, I often find myself stepping into a leadership role — organizing, bringing clarity, and taking action when things feel stuck or uncertain. I'm the kind of person who moves when nobody else does, and helps others move with me.
workEthic.paragraph4: For me, good work is not just about writing code — it's about ownership, consistency, and leaving things better than I found them.

cleanCode.title: Clean code
cleanCode.description: Strong typing, consistent patterns, and readability over cleverness.

testing.title: Test coverage
testing.description: Unit, integration, and E2E tests as a first-class concern, not an afterthought.

observability.title: Observability
observability.description: Proactive monitoring with metrics, logs, and alerting from day one.

collaboration.title: Collaboration
collaboration.description: Clear communication, mentorship, and building inclusive team culture.

personal.title: Beyond the screen
personal.myself:
My life is about balance: doing meaningful work, staying active, and sharing moments with the people that matter most.
Here are a few moments that reflect who I am outside of work:
personal.family:
I live alone in a small apartment in a city called Santa Fe (Argentina), and most weekends I drive to visit my parents.
Family is at the center of my life. I heavily rely on them and they count on me for anything.
We are one strong team. They are my shelter, we share everything; when something good happens, it belongs to all of us; and the same goes for the hard moments.
Growing up in an environment with these people shaped who I am today. It's where I learned the importance of trust, support, and showing up for others, values I carry with me in every aspect of my life.
personal.pets:
We've always had dogs at home; these wonderful creatures mean a lot to me. They are true companions, loyal like no other.
Right now, I share my days with Kira and Shyren. There have been a few others before them though, I'll never forget any of them.
Even something as simple as seeing or petting a dog on the street and getting a tail wag back is enough to make my day better.
personal.travel:
One of the things I enjoy the most is traveling, especially with family and friends.
I love discovering new places, cultures, and environments; but to me, the most important part of any trip is the people you share it with. As I like to say: “It's not really about where you go, but who you go with.”.
My favorite activities so far have been scuba diving and trekking.
personal.disconnection:
Taking time to disconnect is an important part of maintaining balance, especially after long and demanding days.
🧘 😌 🙅‍♂️ To disconnect: I am intentional about reducing distractions by limiting social media use, avoiding doom-scrolling, and keeping notifications under control.
🏋️‍♂️ 🏃‍♂️ ⚽️ To stay active: I train regularly through CrossFit, go running, and occasionally play soccer.
📺 👾 🎧 To entertain myself: I watch movies and TV shows, as well as play video and board games; but, overall, listen to music; putting on my headphones and hitting “play” is fuel for any kind of effort.
